content.full_text:
LAW OFFICES OF PILLSBURY, MADISON & SUTRO

OTHER OFFICES
LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA
SAN DIEGO, CALIFORNIA
SAN JOSE, CALIFORNIA
WALNUT CREEK, CALIFORNIA
WASHINGTON, D.C.

WRITER'S OFFICE AND DIRECT DIAL NUMBER

POST OFFICE BOX 7880
SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA 94120
TELEPHONE (415) 983-1000
CABLE ADDRESS "EVANS"
TELEX 34743
TELECOPIER (415) 398-2096

235 Montgomery Street
(415) 983-1968

October 9, 1990

VIA FEDERAL EXPRESS

Mr. John Wallace
Counsel
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Mr. Wallace:

This letter is a request for informal written advice pursuant to Government Code section 83114(b), confirming your telephone advice of September 24, 1990, to Susan Carey of this office.

This firm represents corporations and business entities, one or more of which may wish to contribute to the Governor-elect's transition team. The donations may consist of the loan of a computer or other office equipment, as well as management or support personnel for a period of time following the November 6, 1990 gubernatorial election. It is anticipated that any equipment or personnel loaned to the Governor-elect's transition team would be used as determined by the team. Expenses for the transition team are paid by the State of California. Gov't Code § 12015; Budget Act of 1990 (Senate Bill No. 899) $2.00, Item 0730-001-001.

It is our understanding that payments in support of the transition team will not be a "gift" under section 82028 and will not qualify as an activity expense under section 86111 if the payments are made within the parameters of the Stone Opinion (3 FPPC Opinions 52, No. 77-003, June 9, 1977). We believe that such donation of equipment and personnel will meet all the criteria for a gift to the State as set forth in the Stone opinion, if:

Mr. John Wallace  
October 9, 1990

(1) The donor intends to donate the use of the equipment and personnel to the State and not to the Governor-elect as an individual.

(2) The Governor-elect and/or the transition team will exercise complete control over use of the equipment and personnel.

(3) Use of the equipment and personnel will be available to the transition team without regard to any member of the team's official status.

(4) The donation will be formalized in a resolution or written contract by the appropriate agency.

Finally, since plans may have to be made before the new Governor is elected, we assume that a corporation may commence the above-described process before the election. Alternatively, we assume that your advice would not change if the donations were made after the governor were elected.

Sincerely,

Teresa C. Lahaderne
